content.full_text:
ROSEMAKERS
PREMIUM QUALITY
CUT ROSES
ESCONDIDO, CALIF.

September 11, 1992

Ms. Luisa Machaca
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" St. #600
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Opinion/ Post Term Restrictions- Del Mar Fair Board Director

Dear Ms. Machaca,

Per our telephone conversation of today, I am formally
requesting from the Commission an opinion regarding what if
any restrictions may be required of me under current law when
my current term expires.

In September of 1983, Governor Deukmejian appointed me
as a Director of the 22nd District Agricultural Association
aka, the Del Mar Fair Board, where I have served continuously.
My term will expire on January 15, 1993.

In January 1985, I was appointed by the Governor to serve
on the State Racetrack Commission where I have served continuously. On January 15, 1993 my term as a Director of the 22nd
Agricultural Association will expire and by law my appointment
to the Racetrack Leasing Commission will terminate simultaneously.

In December 1990, by virtue of my status as a commissioner
on the Racetrack Leasing Commission, I was also appointed
to the Del Mar Racetrack Authority. My appointment to this
State Agency will also terminate simultaneously with the expiration of my term on the Del Mar Fair Board.

Will you kindly advise me as to what if any government
codes will govern my conduct as an attorney in representing
clients who may come before any of these State agencies with
the expressed interest of transacting business of any kind.

Sincerely,

Ray Saatjian

ROSEMAKERS • 9054 Mt. Israel Road • Escondido, California 92029 • (619) 747-5070

sections.question: Will you kindly advise me as to what if any government codes will govern my conduct as an attorney in representing clients who may come before any of these State agencys with the expressed interest of transacting business of any kind.
sections.question_synthetic: What restrictions under government codes will apply to the requester's conduct as an attorney representing clients before state agencies after their terms expire on January 15, 1993, given their simultaneous resignations from the Del Mar Fair Board, State Racetrack Commission, and Del Mar Racetrack Authority?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal Q/A or Conclusion section present. The question is clearly stated in the final paragraph. The document quality is poor with OCR artifacts and formatting issues.

embedding.summary: A requester seeks FPPC guidance on post-employment restrictions that may apply when their terms expire on January 15, 1993, from three state positions: Del Mar Fair Board Director, State Racetrack Commission member, and Del Mar Racetrack Authority appointee. The requester wants to know what government codes will govern their conduct as an attorney representing clients before these agencies.
